description:
You are an infrastructure engineer automating a quick network-diagnostics step that will later be embedded in a provisioning script.  

    Objective:  
      1. Ensure a directory named “/home/user/netdiag” exists.  
      2. Inside that directory, create a text file named “localhost_ping.log”.  
      3. The file must contain exactly one line (terminated with a single newline character) in the following format ‑- nothing more, nothing less:

         packet_loss=0%

    Constraints / verification rules:  
      • The agent should collect the packet-loss value by performing a single-line terminal command that pings the loopback interface (127.0.0.1) exactly once.  
      • The agent must not hard-code the string “0%”; it must be extracted from the command’s own output.  
      • No intermediate files are allowed outside “/home/user/netdiag/”.  
      • The log file must be world-readable (chmod 644) so automated graders can open it.  
      • The test harness will look only for the existence of “/home/user/netdiag/localhost_ping.log” and that its **sole** content is the literal text:

         packet_loss=0%

        followed by a newline character. Any extra whitespace or additional lines will cause failure.  

    Hints (do NOT reveal exact command):  
      • A single pipeline command can perform the ping, isolate the packet-loss field, prepend the literal key “packet_loss=”, and redirect the result into the required file.  
      • Remember to create the directory first if it does not exist, and set the proper permissions on the final log file.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory:
       Path: /home/user/netdiag
       Properties:
         - Must exist.
         - Permissions: 755 (drwxr-xr-x).

    2. File:
       Path: /home/user/netdiag/localhost_ping.log
       Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--).
       Exact content (including newline):
         packet_loss=0%
         ⏎ (single newline character)

    The automated checker will:
       • Verify the directory exists with the exact path and permissions.  
       • Verify the file exists, is a regular file, has permission 644, and its byte-for-byte content is “packet_loss=0%\n”.
